brief.headline: 24.06.2026: data · openai
brief.executive_summary:
Am 24.06.2026 wurden 24 relevante Branchen-Meldungen aus 5 Quellen erfasst. Die thematischen Schwerpunkte lagen bei data · openai.

Die wichtigsten Meldungen des Tages sind nachfolgend kuratiert — sortiert nach Recency und Quellen-Autorität. Vollständige Artikel-Liste unten.

trends.title: Totgesagte leben länger: Wo der Wasserstoffzug doch noch gewinnt
trends.what: In Deutschland fährt der Wasserstoffzug langsam aufs Abstellgleis. Italien lässt 21 Stück bei Stadler bauen - der Schweizer Hersteller feiert die Weltpremiere eines neuen Modells. Hat der H2-Zug jetzt endlich seine Nische gefunden? Der Beitrag Totgesagte leben länger: Wo der Wasserstoffzug doch noch

trends.title: Kühlung von Datenzentren als Goldgrube: KI beschert ebm-Papst kräftiges Wachstum
trends.what: Das Geschäft mit Datenzentren boomt. Davon profitiert auch der Ventilatorenhersteller ebm-Papst, für den auch intern künstliche Intelligenz immer mehr zum Thema wird. raw_articles.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
raw_articles.summary: In its first earnings report since going public, the AI chipmaker forecast a narrower gross margin in its core business, scaring investors.

raw_articles.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
raw_articles.summary: While AI dominates the layoff narrative, engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, according to SignalFire data.

raw_articles.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
raw_articles.summary: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.

raw_articles.title: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this US company
raw_articles.summary: Revenue quadrupled to $41.45 billion compared with the same period a year ago. The company's profit, meanwhile, rose from $1.88 billion to an incredible $28.2 billion year-over-year.

raw_articles.title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
raw_articles.summary: The tokenmaxxing era was brief. We now appear to be entering the era of token rationing.

raw_articles.title: Facebook rolls out an AI companion app for creators
raw_articles.summary: The new app, which is currently being tested with select creators, will have Facebook's recently launched AI creator assistant built into it.

raw_articles.title: Agility Robotics plans to go public via SPAC in a $2.5B deal
raw_articles.summary: Agility Robotics, the humanoid robotics startup that spun out of Oregon State University in 2015, expects to generate $620 million in proceeds.

raw_articles.title: Figma adds code layers, support for animations, more AI features in new update
raw_articles.summary: Figma's update adds a new code layer, support for motion and shaders, and the ability to create custom plug-ins for various tasks using AI.

raw_articles.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
raw_articles.summary: Named Jalapeño, the new processor was designed specifically for the unique needs of OpenAI's inference systems.

raw_articles.title: The emergence of the web data infrastructure layer for AI
raw_articles.summary: AI is booming. New use cases are emerging each day. To capitalize on the technology’s potential, enterprises require data at scale. In many cases, though, the relevant information is blocked or unstructured, which limits its use by AI models.  To understand this challenge, consider the foundation of the web itself.
